title: waltheria lanceolata
intro: Waltheria lanceolata, also known as West Indian nettle or bitter leaf, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. Waltheria lanceolata has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
